title: Orbital Lifeboats: Napkin Math for a Solar-System Emergency-Cache and Rescue Network
description: An engineering-constraints exploration of pre-positioned emergency resource caches across the solar system — the WWII English Channel rescue buoy reimagined for spaceflight. The central reframing: in orbit, rescue distance is measured in delta-v, not meters; a stranded spacecraft is a ballistic object locked on its conic, not a swimmer. The repository contains the working paper, Python models for reachability and cache-placement napkin math, and generated figures. Explicitly exploratory: big-number napkin math, not a mission proposal. Text and figures are licensed CC BY 4.0; code is licensed MIT.
